description: try_vault_path uses raw_path.starts_with('/') as the absolute-path check. This is Unix-only. On Windows, absolute paths are 'C:\Users\foo' or '\\?\C:\foo' or '\\server\share' — none start with '/'. Windows users would receive VaultPathError::NotAbsolute for every legitimate path, making the app unusable on Windows. The behavioral-spec edge-case catalog mentions OS-portability concerns (EC-15 OS_PATH_MAX, EC-14 symlinks) but does not include a Windows-path edge case, and there is no test exercising Windows-style paths. Tauri 2 + SvelteKit + Bun is a cross-platform desktop stack — Windows compatibility is implicit. The Rust validation should use std::path::Path::is_absolute() or delegate to the existing VaultPath::try_new in promptnotes/src-tauri/src/domain/value_objects.rs (which would handle this correctly via the canonical implementation).
